title: Brand Manager
location: Greater London, Greater London, England, United Kingdom
description:
Brand Manager

London, Marylebone (Hybrid) - Creams CafÃ©

Brands build awareness. Great brands drive sales.

At Creams CafÃ©, we're looking for a commercially focused Brand Manager who understands that successful marketing isn't just about creative campaignsâit's about increasing guest visits, driving sales, growing market share, and delivering measurable business results.

Reporting to the Chief Marketing Officer, you'll take ownership of national brand campaigns and local store marketing initiatives across our growing franchise network, ensuring every activity contributes to brand growth and commercial performance.

This role is ideal for someone who loves analysing performance, spotting opportunities, working closely with operational teams and franchise partners, and turning marketing activity into real revenue growth.


What You'll Be Responsible For
  • Driving sales through impactfull national marketing campaigns and local Cafe marketing initiatives.
  • Working closely with franchise partners to identify opportunities to increase footfall, guest frequency and average transaction value.
  • Developing targeted local marketing plans that deliver measurable results within individual cafÃ©s and trading areas.
  • Analysing campaign performance, ROI and commercial KPIs, providing clear recommendations to improve results.
  • Identifying partnerships and promotional opportunities that drive awareness and generate revenue.
  • Using market trends, customer insight and competitor activity to shape future campaigns and business decisions.
  • Protecting and growing the Creams brand while ensuring all activity supports commercial objectives.
What We're Looking For
  • 5+ years' experience in Brand Marketing, Local Store Marketing or Commercial Marketing.
  • Experience within hospitality, retail, leisure, franchising, QSR or food & beverage brands.
  • A proven track record of delivering marketing campaigns that have directly driven sales growth.
  • Strong analytical skills with experience interpreting data, KPIs and campaign performance.
  • Commercially minded with an understanding of unit economics, customer acquisition and revenue growth.
  • Highly organised, hands-on and comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously.


Why Join Creams?

We're one of the UK's leading dessert brands with ambitious growth plans, a strong franchise network and a passion for creating unforgettable guest experiences.

This is an opportunity to take ownership of marketing activity that genuinely impacts business performance, work directly with franchise partners, and help shape the future of a growing national brand.

If you can combine creativity with commercial thinking and understand that marketing should ultimately drive sales, we'd love to hear from you.

Immediate start would be ideal

location: London Area, London Area, United Kingdom
description:
Brand & Marketing Director

London based hybrid working (3 days remote)

Permanent

Â£130,000 to Â£140,000 salary (DOE) + bonus + benefits

PM-50845


Role requires a Brand & Marketing Director with a proven track record scaling luxury or premium fashion brands internationally, and overseeing Creative & Design, PR & Influencer, Marketing, Social, Content, and Brand Activation.


Our client is one of the quietly compelling names in modern British luxury. A house built around beautifully crafted leather accessories, made ethically by trusted ateliers, with a founding commitment to giving back at its emotional heart. Worn by tastemakers, editors and cultural figures across the world, the brand has grown deliberately and with discipline, with the founder still at its creative centre. They're now entering a new retail chapter, with an expanded international presence and an evolving global brand platform.


Reporting to the Managing Director and working closely with the Founder, you'll own the global brand, marketing and communications strategy, defining and evolving the brand vision, visual identity and creative direction across every channel. You'll set the creative standard across campaigns, photography, content and communications, and lead a high-performing team spanning Creative & Design, PR & Influencer, Marketing, Social, Content, and Brand Activation.


Responsibilities:

  • Own global brand strategy, positioning and creative direction
  • Lead integrated marketing campaigns across all channels
  • Oversee PR, influencer partnerships and brand activations
  • Drive content, social and communications strategy
  • Use customer insight to shape brand direction and influence product development
  • Partner closely with Product, E-commerce, Retail and Wholesale to ensure brand consistency throughout the customer journey
  • Build, mentor and inspire a multidisciplinary team


Experience sought:

  • Director, VP or C-Suite level brand and marketing leadership within luxury or premium fashion or lifestyle
  • Proven track record building and scaling brands across international markets
  • Experience leading creative direction and integrated marketing for a premium or luxury brand
  • Deep expertise across Creative & Design, PR & Influencer, Marketing, Social, Content, and Brand Activation
  • An inspiring, collaborative leader with experience building high-performing teams
  • Genuine passion for craftsmanship, sustainability and the evolving luxury landscape
  • Experience with adoption of emerging technologies to enhance creativity, marketing performance 


Send through your CV with a summary of your experience through to hello@wearepeas.com

title: Freelance Designer
description:
About Us

We prioritize hiring individuals based on their core values rather than solely focusing on hard skills. Our approach is to empower exceptional candidates to excel in a role by leveraging their strengths, regardless of whether they precisely match a job description. As a result, we welcome applicants with varying levels of experience, both exceeding and falling short of the requirements outlined below.

PixelPiew is a privately owned studio specializing in design, branding, and creative services. Our mission is to drive the evolution and prosperity of financial services, B2B, corporate, and non-profit organizations. We achieve this by harnessing the power of research and craftsmanship to create refined brand identities, websites, messaging, campaigns, and marketing materials.

We Want

We Are Actively Seeking Skilled And Enthusiastic Freelance Designers To Join Our Roster Of Creative Professionals. This Is a Broad Application, And We Invite All Designers To Apply, With a Special Emphasis On Those Who Excel In The Following Areas:

  • Logo and Brand Design
  • Motion Graphics Design
  • UX Design
  • Print Collateral Design
  • PowerPoint/Google Slides Presentation Design

Please be aware that while we may not be able to respond to every applicant immediately, we will retain all submissions for potential future opportunities. If you are passionate about design and are eager to tackle exciting and demanding projects, we sincerely encourage you to reach out to us!

Compensation

This is a freelance, remote position, but you are also welcome to work on-site at our Providence, RI location if you prefer. We offer competitive compensation that aligns with your level of expertise and experience.

How to Apply

To apply, please email careers@pixelpier.com, including your resume and portfolio. To test your attention to detail and show that youâve read this entire listing, please also share your favorite typeface and explain why it resonates with you.

title: Designer Residential
description:
NORR is a global team of 800 architects, engineers, planners, and interior designers creating design strategies and solutions that express the unique vision of every project. Driven by a common purpose with our clients and partners, we share ideas to create and innovate, bringing people, design, and technologies together to think bigger and act bolder.

Our story began in 1938 and has evolved into an employee-owned, fully integrated firm committed to sustainable development goals across 13 market sectors in Canada, the US, and UK. Design excellence continues to guide everything we do.

At NORR, our residential portfolio spans decades and project types. Our approach to multi-family residential design supports smart growth and walkable neighborhoods with building wellness initiatives, integrated carbon-reduction strategies, innovative construction technologies, and resilient infrastructure. Every solution is guided by principles of design excellence, urban regeneration, inclusive context, and marketability.

Learn more about our Residential Portfolio.

As a Designer, you will work with other members of the design team in order to develop design solutions appropriate to the context, budget and aesthetic requirements of architectural projects. You will collaborate with other professional disciplines and have opportunities to coordinate the design through all phases of the project.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Compiles information from reports and published documents; evaluates and analyzes the information to understand broad requirements of project
  • Develops architectural designs, to ensure an exemplary solution is delivered that meets quality parameters, project timelines and budgetary requirements
  • Coordinates design intent with other disciplines
  • Integrates engineering requirements, site surveys and field review information, adhering to applicable codes and jurisdictional requirements
  • Prepares design reports, drawings and models following office standards
  • Performs site visitations to determine compliance with construction documents; identifies any deviations and liaises with owner and contactor to resolve issues in a timely manner
  • Reviews project products, services, and shop drawings to ensure adherence to project documents, relevant jurisdictional codes and corporate quality controls

Required Skills

  • Experience in all phases of the project cycle is required
  • Architectural design software skills including Revit, AutoCAD, SketchUp, PhotoShop, and other architectural design software
  • Familiarity with MEP and Structural building systems and building codes is required
  • Have successfully completed related post-secondary education
  • Registered with a relevant professional architectural association is an asset

Required Experience

  • 7-10 years of project work experience
  • Experience in Residential projects is an asset
  • Salary Range: $70,000.00 - $105,000.00 /annually

title: Customer Experience Specialist
description:
At DraftKings, AI is becoming an integral part of both our present and future, powering how work gets done today, guiding smarter decisions, and sparking bold ideas. Itâs transforming how we enhance customer experiences, streamline operations, and unlock new possibilities. Our teams are energized by innovation and readily embrace emerging technology. Weâre not waiting for the future to arrive. Weâre shaping it, one bold step at a time. To those who see AI as a driver of progress, come build the future together.

The Crown Is Yours

As a Customer Experience Specialist, youâll help deliver exceptional customer experiences by partnering closely with our vendors and internal teams. Youâll support the day-to-day success of our outsourced operations, helping ensure customers receive consistent, high-quality support across every interaction. Working across Customer Experience, Shared Services, Analytics, Risk, and Technology teams, youâll solve operational challenges, identify opportunities for improvement, and drive performance that enhances the customer experience.

What you'll do

  • Support the operational performance of Customer Experience vendors, helping ensure service level objectives, quality standards, and customer satisfaction goals are consistently achieved.
  • Serve as a key partner to vendor teams by resolving operational questions, coordinating cross-functional support, and helping remove obstacles that impact performance and the customer experience.
  • Review customer interactions, operational trends, and performance metrics to identify improvement opportunities, provide actionable feedback, and support continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Facilitate calibration sessions, performance reviews, associate huddles, and operational communications to reinforce quality expectations, share updates, and drive alignment across teams.
  • Support operational escalations and customer-impacting incidents by coordinating responses, monitoring emerging risks and performance trends, and partnering with stakeholders to drive timely resolution.


What you'll bring

  • A Bachelorâs Degree in Business, Communications, Operations, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and professional experience.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to build effective relationships across internal teams and external partners.
  • Excellent analytical, critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.

title: Rippling
description:
The verdict

Rippling is one of the strongest products you can buy if you run HR, IT, and finance as connected systems, and the review data backs that up: 4.8 across more than 14,000 G2 reviews and 4.9 on Capterra. The onboarding automation that provisions payroll, benefits, IT access, and a device in one move is the standout, and it earns its keep. Go in clear-eyed about two things: the modular, quote-based pricing stacks faster than people expect, and the first-week learning curve is real.

Key features

One employee record that drives everything

Hire someone once and the record propagates everywhere: payroll, benefits, device provisioning, app access, and offboarding all read from it, so a department change updates downstream automatically.

Automated payroll and tax compliance

Rippling calculates payroll taxes and files them with the correct federal, state, and local agencies, supports multi-entity and global payroll for employees and contractors, and stores electronic W-2s and 1099s.

One-click onboarding and offboarding

A single flow sends offer letters, enrolls payroll and benefits, ships a device, and grants app logins; offboarding runs it in reverse, revoking access and triggering device returns.

Benefits administration with payroll sync

Over 500 EDI and API carrier integrations, integrated HSA/FSA/commuter accounts with automatic payroll-deduction sync, COBRA and ACA automation, and bring-your-own-broker support alongside Rippling's PEO and brokerage.

600+ out-of-the-box integrations

Connects QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Guideline for 401(k), Checkr for background checks, plus identity and productivity tools, with real user provisioning, attribute mapping, and cross-app workflow automation.

Modular HR, IT, and Finance products

Buy the core Rippling Platform, then add only the products you need across HR (Payroll, Benefits, Learning, Performance), IT (Identity, Device, Inventory), and Finance (Corporate Cards, Expenses, Bill Pay).

What it is

Rippling is an HR platform that doesnât stop at HR. The core is a single employee record: hire someone once, and that record drives payroll, benefits, device provisioning, app access, and offboarding. Where most HR software treats the employee database as a system of record, Rippling treats it as a trigger. Change a personâs department and Rippling updates it everywhere downstream. Onboard a new hire and it enrolls them in payroll, sets up their benefits, ships a laptop, and grants the right app logins from the same flow.

The product is sold in modules. Thereâs a core, required Rippling Platform, and then you buy what you need on top of it: HR products (Payroll, Benefits, Learning, Performance, Recruiting, Time & Attendance), IT products (Identity & Access, Device, Inventory), and Finance products (Corporate Cards, Expenses, Bill Pay). The pitch, and itâs a real one, is that all three sit on the same data, so the IT and finance sides arenât bolted-on afterthoughts.

Who itâs for

This is built for the company that has outgrown a point HR tool but is also tired of running payroll in one place, device management in another, and expense reports in a third. If youâre hiring across multiple states or countries, Ripplingâs strongest. It runs multi-entity and global payroll, stores W-2s and 1099s, and handles contractors through dedicated products. Reviewers who pay people internationally describe currency conversion and cross-border pay as something Rippling âjust takes care of.â

Itâs a worse fit for a simple operation. If you have fifteen domestic employees, one benefits plan, and no real IT footprint, the all-in-one model is overkill, and reviewers say exactly that, warning that simpler teams risk overbuying. Youâd be paying for breadth you wonât use and absorbing a configuration burden you donât need. Rippling rewards companies that genuinely run HR, IT, and finance as connected systems. If those live in separate departments that never talk, you lose the main reason to buy it.

Why it stands out

Onboarding and offboarding is the standout, and itâs not close. Capterra logs 97% positive mentions on onboarding, with reviewers describing clear step-by-step guidance to add employees, configure payroll, and connect benefits. business.com calls it a major strength. The thing people keep describing is âone-click onboardingâ that provisions payroll, IT access, and a device in a single action, and the same automation runs in reverse at offboarding, revoking access and triggering device returns. The January 2026 release added advanced Microsoft 365 offboarding actions, which is exactly the kind of detail that matters when someone leaves.

The unified platform is the second genuine win. Capterra users rate the all-in-one dashboard 98% positive, and reviewers repeatedly call out Ripplingâs UI as the best theyâve used in an HRMS. Collapsing payroll, benefits, and people data into one synced record removes the manual re-entry and the reconciliation errors that plague stitched-together stacks.

Integration breadth is the third. Rippling ships 600+ (the FAQ says 650+) out-of-the-box integrations: QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Guideline for 401(k), Checkr for background checks, plus the usual identity and productivity tools, with real provisioning and attribute mapping, not just data export. Reviewers call this exceptional. The one caveat worth flagging: Expert Market notes Rippling doesnât natively integrate with third-party HR systems, so the strength is in app provisioning and internal cross-module sync rather than swapping out parts of the HR stack.

Pricing in plain language

Rippling starts around $8 per employee per month, but that number tells you very little. The real model is modular and quote-based: thereâs no full price list on the site, and some products carry a monthly base fee on top of the per-employee rate. You buy the core platform, then add each product separately. Thatâs the flexibility selling point and the budgeting headache in one.

Hereâs where the wall is: the modules stack. Independent reviewers are consistent on this, the per-employee base is reasonable, but as you add Benefits, Device Management, Expenses, and the rest, the total climbs in ways that arenât obvious upfront. The most common complaint across G2, Capterra, and third-party reviews isnât that Rippling is bad value, itâs that the total cost is hard to see coming. Benefits administration is a particular gotcha: itâs a separate paid module, which is why business.com calls benefits a ânotable weaknessâ on cost grounds, though those fees can be waived if Rippling is your broker or PEO.

Get a real quote before you fall in love with the demo, and price the modules youâll actually turn on, not the ones that look nice in the sales deck.

Limitations

The learning curve is real. The same feature density that makes Rippling powerful makes it overwhelming on day one. G2 reviewers describe it as a lot to absorb, especially for new admins. This is the cost of the all-in-one model, and itâs worth budgeting setup time for.

Stability is the more concerning one. Capterra reviewers flag platform bugs and slow loads (48% negative) and auth or session timeouts (46% negative), and the mobile app comes up as occasionally buggy and missing features the desktop version has. More seriously, payroll, the thing you most need to be boring and correct, has edge cases. Capterra reviewers report incorrect overtime calculations that forced them to claw back overpayments, and Thrivea notes tax edge-case issues. The payroll engine is rated highly overall, with a 100% error-free filing guarantee cited by business.com, but the exceptions are the kind you remember.

Reporting is the quieter gap. 43% of Capterra mentions on reporting are negative, centered on a lack of customization. If you need to slice your people data in non-standard ways, expect to fight the report builder. Benefits enrollment is the last one worth naming: Thrivea reports the workflows can feel clunky or incomplete, which is part of why benefits sentiment runs mixed even though the payroll-to-deduction sync is well liked.

The bottom line

If youâre a growing company running HR, IT, and finance as connected systems, especially across states or borders, Rippling is one of the strongest products you can buy, and the review data backs that up. The onboarding automation alone earns its keep, and the unified record genuinely removes work. Buy it for the integration, not just the HR.

But go in clear-eyed. Get a real quote and add up every module youâll switch on, because the stacking is where people get surprised. Budget setup time for the learning curve. And if youâre a small, single-country team with simple benefits and no IT footprint, this is more platform than you need, youâll pay for breadth you wonât touch. Rippling rewards complexity. Bring it the right kind, and itâs excellent.

What people are saying online

Rippling is one of the most highly rated HR platforms I track, 4.8 across more than 14,000 G2 reviews and 4.9 on Capterra. Users love the unified dashboard and the way onboarding fires off payroll, benefits, and IT setup in one move. The platform is most positive for growing companies that genuinely run HR, IT, and finance together and have the bandwidth to configure it. The recurring frustrations are the same ones every time: a steep first-week learning curve, occasional bugs and slow loads, and a modular price tag that stacks up faster than people expect.

Drawn from independent reviews and discussions, separate from our methodology score.

What people love

  • Onboarding logs 97% positive mentions, one click provisions payroll, benefits, IT access, and ships the device
  • Capterra
  • All-in-one platform rated 98% positive for collapsing payroll, benefits, and people data into one synced record
  • Capterra
  • Cited as the best HRMS UI reviewers have used, intuitive and genuinely modern
  • Multiple
  • Direct deposit (97%) and payroll entry (95%) rated highly; international pay 'just gets taken care of'
  • G2
  • 600+ third-party app integrations praised as exceptional for provisioning and cross-app automation
  • Independent reviews

Common complaints

  • Sheer number of features overwhelms new users; admin configuration carries a real learning curve
  • G2
  • Platform stability complaints, bugs and slow loads (48% negative) plus auth/session timeouts (46% negative)
  • Capterra
  • Reporting lacks customization (43% negative)
  • Capterra
  • Payroll edge cases bite, including incorrect overtime calculations that forced overpayment recovery
  • Multiple
  • Opaque, quote-based pricing with add-ons that 'stack' into surprise total cost
  • Multiple

Rippling alternatives

#2

Gusto

3.9

#3

Deel

3.9

#4

TriNet

3.6

Where Rippling ranks

  • HR Platforms #1 of 6 3.9

Best for ops-heavy and multi-country teams that want HR, IT, and finance running off one synced employee record.

title: Receptionist
location: Massapequa, Massapequa, New York, United States
description:
Job Title: Remote Therapist Receptionist

Location: Remote (EST)

Employment Type: Full-Time

About The Role

Evolve Psychiatry is seeking a compassionate, organized, and customer-service-oriented Remote Therapist Receptionist to support our growing team of licensed therapists. This individual serves as the primary administrative point of contact for therapy patients, ensuring a seamless experience from initial inquiry through ongoing treatment.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, empathetic, comfortable working in a fast-paced healthcare environment, and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining exceptional patient care. This role requires excellent communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently in a fully remote environment.

Responsibilities:

  • Answer phone calls and route inquiries appropriately
  • Schedule, confirm and modify appointments using our EMR system
  • Verify insurance information and collect copays
  • Communicate efficiently with therapists, patients, and other staff
  • Handle sensitive information with discretion and confidentiality

Qualifications:

  • Previous experience in a medical receptionist role working directly with therapists preferred
  • Experience in psychiatry or behavioral health
  • Proficient with electronic medical records and general computer skills
  • Strong organizational abilities and multitasking skills within a remote environment
  • Professional appearance/demeanor
  • Experience working in a fastpaced administrative environment
  • Strong computer proficiency

Success in This Role:The Ideal candidate is someone who:

  • Enjoys helping patients and providing outstanding customer service.
  • Can confidently manage a high volume of phone calls and appointment scheduling.
  • Thrives in a fastpaced, remote environment.
  • Pays close attention to detail and follows processes consistently.
  • Communicates professionally with patients, therapists, and other team members.
  • Takes initiative and is comfortable solving problems independently.

Why Join Us?

  • Be a part of a respected and growing psychiatry practice
  • Work in a collaborative, missiondriven environment
  • Competitive pay and benefits package
  • Make a meaningful difference in patients' lives everday

title: Head of People
description:
About Bizee

Bizee has helped nearly two million entrepreneurs start and run their businesses. Now we are building what comes next: an AI-powered Entrepreneur Operating System that helps founders launch, operate, stay compliant, and grow with less friction and more confidence.

We are profitable, founder-led, and operating at a clear inflection point. The next chapter requires a stronger operating system for how we hire, lead, communicate, develop talent, and scale execution across the company.

The Opportunity

We are looking for a Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness to serve as a strategic partner to the President and executive team.

This is not a maintenance HR role. It is a builder-operator role for someone who can create clarity, raise the talent bar, strengthen the leadership layer, and build the people systems that help Bizee scale without unnecessary complexity or organizational drag.

As Bizee grows and transforms, one of the most important responsibilities of this role will be building and protecting a high talent bar. We are not simply adding headcount; we are intentionally increasing talent density across the company. This leader will help ensure we hire high-caliber builders, operators, and leaders who raise the standard, strengthen execution, and fit the culture we are building.

You will help design how Bizee hires, onboards, develops, manages, communicates, and performs as we move into our next stage of growth.

What You Will Own

  • Partner with the President and executive team on org design, leadership effectiveness, talent quality, succession, performance, and cross-functional friction
  • Design and protect Bizee's talent bar. Build the hiring standards, role scorecards, interview calibration, decision rules, and leadership alignment required to consistently identify high-caliber talent as the company scales. Ensure we do not compromise on quality-of-hire in the name of speed
  • Design a scalable hiring system, including workforce planning, role definition, scorecards, interview loops, calibration, decision frameworks, and candidate experience
  • Build Bizee's People Operating System, including the rhythms, mechanisms, and rituals that drive alignment, accountability, and performance
  • Strengthen the manager layer through practical enablement, feedback systems, performance routines, and leadership expectations
  • Design and own Bizee's performance cycle: goal setting, ratings, calibration, and linkage to comp and promotion decisions
  • Advise on role clarity, leveling, compensation philosophy, incentive design, spans, layers, and capability planning
  • Build onboarding, engagement, and internal communication mechanisms that help employees ramp faster and stay connected to the mission
  • Lead change management as Bizee modernizes its platform, operating model, AI-enabled workflows, and distributed teams
  • Plan and execute the eventual transition away from full co-employment (PEO) arrangements in multiple countries globally, including building the internal HRIS, payroll, benefits and compliance infrastructure needed to stand up direct employment, sequencing the migration to minimize risk and disruption, and managing the associated legal, tax, and employee communication considerations.
  • Own multi-state wage and hour compliance and workforce administration for a distributed employee population spanning nearly all 50 U.S. states, encompassing both exempt and non-exempt staff
  • Lead and integrate teams across multiple countries internationally, ensuring a single coherent Bizee culture and set of operating standards translate effectively across geographies, management structures, and local labor norms

Requirements

What You Will Bring

  • 10+ years of progressive experience in People, Talent, HRBP, Organizational Effectiveness, or People Operations leadership
  • Experience building People systems from the ground up, ideally in a high-growth, profitable, founder-led, PE-backed, SaaS, platform, marketplace, or tech-enabled services environment
  • Strong executive judgment and the ability to operate as a true strategic partner to a President and executive team
  • Proven experience with hiring systems, talent calibration, performance management, manager enablement, org design, leveling, compensation frameworks, and People Ops infrastructure
  • A strong point of view on talent density, quality-of-hire, leadership standards, and what it takes to build a high-performance company
  • Strong operator instincts: structured, practical, clear, and biased toward action
  • High EQ and high backbone: able to coach leaders, challenge assumptions, handle sensitive issues, and create clarity without adding drama or bureaucracy
  • Strategic and hands-on: able to advise the executive team in the morning and fix an onboarding workflow in the afternoon
  • Business-first orientation: understands revenue, margin, productivity, execution, and the tradeoffs of scaling a profitable company
  • Low-drama, high-signal communication style: brings facts, judgment, candor, and calm to complex situations
  • Culture carrier: protects what makes Bizee special while raising the performance bar
  • Comfort operating in ambiguity and building while the business is moving fast
  • Strong written communication skills and the ability to turn messy organizational issues into simple plans and operating mechanisms
  • Demonstrated experience managing a multi-state U.S. workforce (ideally 40+ states) that includes both exempt and non-exempt employees, with working knowledge of state-by-state wage and hour variation and how to operationalize compliance without adding unnecessary drag
  • Experience leading or partnering with teams in multiple countries, with a track record of integrating culture, standards, and management practices across borders rather than running parallel, disconnected functions
  • Direct experience transitioning a workforce out of a co-employment/PEO model, including standing up in-country or in-state direct employment infrastructure (payroll, benefits, statutory compliance) and managing the change process for affected employees and managers

What Success Looks Like

  • In the first year, Bizee has a stronger People Operating System that improves alignment, ownership, speed, and accountability across the company
  • Hiring is more structured, faster, and higher-signal. Leaders know what great looks like by role and level, hiring decisions are more disciplined, and the company is increasing talent density as it scales
  • Managers are better equipped to set direction, coach performance, handle conflict, and create clarity. Employees have stronger expectations, better communication, and a clearer understanding of how their work connects to the company's mission

The Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness becomes a trusted advisor to the President, executive team, and leadership layer, helping Bizee scale with discipline, clarity, high standards, and good judgment.

Benefits

Disclaimer:

Bizee is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Bizee is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

The compensation offered to the successful applicant may vary based on factors including experience, skills, education, location, and other job-related reasons.

This employer participates in E-verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S.

Available benefits include: Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision), Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D), Virtual Wellness Resources, Work From Home

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to invest in you, and in the things you care about. Your health. Your well-being. Your security. Your future. title: General Labour
description:
Come Build Your Career at Aecon!

Aecon delivers some of the most complex and impactful infrastructure projects â from transformative transportation networks to critical energy, industrial and nuclear programs. The projects we build connect communities and power future generations. With deep roots in North America and a strong footprint internationally, Aecon brings global expertise and proudly serves public and private sector clients through its Construction and Concessions segment.

Safety Always is not only our #1 core value â it is the standard that anchors our culture. We believe the most ambitious projects deserve the most committed people. At Aecon, you wonât just build your career â youâll help build what matters to enable future generations to thrive.

At Aecon, You Can Count On

  • Safety Always. Our number one core value. The safety of our people, projects, partners, and stakeholders is our priority focus â today and always.
  • Integrity. We lead by example, with humility and courage.
  • Accountability. Weâre passionate about delivering on our commitments.
  • Inclusion. We provide opportunities where people feel valued, supported, and empowered to contribute fully.


We deliver infrastructure with purpose, and our people are at the heart of everything we do. Aecon employees are incredibly proud to build some of the most impactful infrastructure of this generation â we call it Aecon Proud.

At Aecon We

  • Ensure you and your family receive the services and benefits needed to support your mental, emotional, and physical well-being.
  • Are intentional when it comes to investing in your development. We help you build your career and advance your skills through our Aecon University, tuition reimbursement, and Leadership Programs.
  • Are committed to creating work environments focused on mutual respect, teamwork, collaboration, and new ideas, through meaningful initiatives, training, partnerships with Veteran groups, our Aecon Women in Trades and diversity programs, as well as our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), to ensure we are building with top talent and harnessing our collective strengths within every aspect of our culture.
  • Operate responsibly by managing risk, safety, and environmental considerations across all our projects and surrounding communities.


Our success is built on the passion, expertise, and dedication of our people. Together, we embrace strong execution, innovation, and continuous improvement â values that come to life through the unique talents and collaborative spirit of every team member. If youâre inspired to make a difference through future-building projects, join our best-in-class team.

What is the Opportunity?

Aeconâs Civil East sector is a cornerstone of our construction and infrastructure operations, delivering innovative solutions to some of the most complex and impactful projects globally. Renowned for its expertise in heavy civil construction, our Civil East sector specializes in building essential infrastructure that powers economies and enhances communities.

In the Caribbean region, we are leveraging our expertise and collaborative approach to deliver world-class infrastructure projects tailored to meet the unique needs of the island communities.

We are looking for a General Labourer to support and assist the team in building construction, especially with set up, clean up and ensuring a safe working environment.

What Youâll Do Here

  • Entry level position, we are willing to train the right candidate - must be able to follow instructions, have a strong work ethic, reliable, and self-motivated.
  • Ability to lift 50 lbs. on a regular basis and stand/walk for long periods of time.
  • Ability to communicate with supervisors, co-workers, contractors and the general public in a courteous, professional manner at all times.
  • Prepare and clean up construction sites.
  • Move materials and equipment and perform demolition, excavation and compaction activities.
  • Handle and distribute construction materials (e.g. load and unload vehicles with supplies, equipment and construction materials; move tools, equipment and construction materials to and from work areas; remove rubble and other debris).
  • Spot for equipment operators when required.
  • General site maintenance.


What You Bring To The Team

  • Physical strength and stamina
  • Manual dexterity
  • A safety-conscious attitude
  • The ability to work with others in a team


Aecon fosters belonging within and across our organization. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and considering all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws.

title: Finance & HR Specialist
location: San Antonio, San Antonio, Texas Metropolitan Area
description:
Job Summary:

The Finance & HR Specialist serves as a key member of the Business Operations team, owning the day-to-day financial, payroll, and people operations functions of the organization. This role is responsible for ensuring accuracy, organization, and efficiency across invoicing, billing quality control, employee and contractor administration, payroll support, and HR operations. This individual partners closely with external partners to ensure smooth financial operations while supporting the employee and contractor lifecycle from onboarding through offboarding.


Duties/Responsibilities:

Financial Operations

  • Own the invoicing and billing quality control process to ensure accuracy and completeness prior to invoice submission
  • Review timesheets, bill rates, pay rates, markups, expenses, and billing data for accuracy
  • Coordinate invoice corrections and resolve billing discrepancies with internal teams
  • Serve as the primary operational liaison with the Companyâs external accounting and finance partners
  • Support payroll processing activities and payroll audits
  • Assist with financial reporting requests
  • Maintain financial records, reports, and supporting documentation
  • Process and track employee and contractor expense reimbursements
  • Support workersâ compensation audits, insurance audits, and client audit requests
  • Facilitate and own accounts receivable follow-up and collections activities

Payroll Administration

  • Support payroll processing activities and payroll quality control reviews
  • Ensure accurate collection and validation of timekeeping records
  • Process payroll changes including new hires, terminations, compensation changes, deductions, and garnishments
  • Serve as the point of contact with payroll-related inquiries from employees and contractors
  • Support the implementation and administration of an internal payroll function

HR Operations and Program Support

  • Coordinate employee and contractor onboarding and offboarding activities
  • Administer background checks, employment verification requests, and onboarding documentation
  • Maintain employee personnel files and HR documentation in compliance with company policies and applicable laws
  • Administer and coordinate benefits administration activities including enrollments, changes, and qualifying life events
  • Support open enrollment activities and communications
  • Track policy acknowledgements, required training, and compliance requirements
  • Assist with unemployment claims administration and workersâ compensation documentation
  • Maintain HRIS data integrity and employee records
  • Support the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of HR programs, policies, and processes
  • Coordinate employee engagement initiatives, recognition programs, and culture initiatives
  • Support special projects and strategic initiatives as assigned by leadership
  • Perform other duties as assigned


Qualifications/Requirements:

  • Associateâs or Bachelorâs degree in Business Administration, Human Resources, Accounting, Finance, or a related field preferred
  • 3+ years of experience in accounting administration, payroll administration, human resources administration, business operations, or a related role
  • Experience owning and supporting invoicing, billing, payroll, accounts payable, accounts receivable, or financial administration activities
  • Experience supporting employee onboarding, benefits administration, personnel records management, and other HR administrative functions
  • Experience working with HRIS, payroll, accounting, or ERP systems.
  • Experience working with QuickBooks
  • Experience supporting multi-state employees and contractors preferred
  • Experience within staffing, professional services, consulting, or project-based organizations preferred


Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:

  • Ability to work independently and in a team environment
  • Excellent organizational and project management skills
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to prioritize multiple competing deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Knowledge of payroll processes and practices
  • Knowledge of general accounting principles and financial administrative practices
  • Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise sound judgement
  • Strong customer service and relationship management skills
  • Ability to identify discrepancies and resolve issues proactively
  • Ability to document and improve processes and workflows
  • Ability to establish priorities and demonstrate a strong sense of urgency
  • Ability to adapt to changing business priorities and organizational needs
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities simultaneously while meeting deadlines
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and reporting tools


Benefits:

  • Dental insurance
  • Health insurance
  • Health savings account
  • Life insurance
  • Paid time off
  • Retirement plan

Fully Remote - Work from anywhere ð

Flexible hours - Choose your own schedule â°

We're looking for the best managers the UK has to offer! If you like the idea of taking control of your earnings, keep reading...


About us:

Grail Talent is an Influencer Management Agency that connects our diverse and carefully curated roster of content creators with digital marketing opportunities, working with brands and record labels to advertise products and music on social media through influencer promotion and performance marketing.

We work with some of the biggest players in Music and New Media, including Sony Music Entertainment and Amazon Prime Video, to help promote the next viral sensation or hit show. In doing so, we are shaping the future of how digital marketing budgets are spent; delivering dynamic and creative promotional campaigns across a variety of social media channels such as TikTok and Instagram, and measuring their impact with detailed analytics.


Job Summary:

As an Influencer Talent Manager, you will be responsible for managing the careers of our creators, helping them grow their audience and monetise their content. Using our in-house software and your own expertise, you will also be responsible for negotiating deals with brands/record labels and developing content strategies for our clients.

About you:

Bonus: Experience with talent management, social media influencers, sales or marketing agencies!



Why Join Grail Talent?

  • Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy flexibility with remote work options and the ability to manage your own schedule.
  • Creative & Collaborative Culture: Work with a dynamic, passionate team that values innovation and creativity.


Ready to Make an Impact?

If youâre excited about shaping the careers of Instagram influencers and want to help lead the future of influencer marketing, weâd love to hear from you! Please submit your resume along with a cover letter detailing your experience and qualifications.

title: Social Media Content Creator
description:
Company Description


Here's the problem we're obsessed with: when you want to do something in your free time, you open TikTok or you ask ChatGPT and both send you to the exact same place as everyone else. The same viral pasta spot, the same rooftop, the same top-10 list. The algorithm flattens a whole city into five places and funnels everyone into them.


But there's no single Milan! There are as many as there are pairs of eyes looking at it, and yours looks nothing like the top-10 everyone else gets fed. Hic finds your version of the city and lets you live it in one tap. We're live in Milan, Florence, Berlin, London, Madrid, and Zurich.


Our content fights the same fight: not "5 things to do in Milan" (that's the enemy), but the specific, the hidden, the strange. Restaurants worth crossing town for, museums that aren't boring, bars, corners, and the stories nobody told you. The stuff that makes someone feel like the city was built for them. That's what you'd be making.




Role Description


We don't want to hand you a template. We want someone to own our TikTok voice and run with it: your ideas, your style, your hooks. You'll have the creative freedom to make people fall in love with Hic, however you see it, working hand in hand with the founder. You'd make around 4 short videos a week, with room to grow: shoot Milan on the ground, and edit footage for the other cities. This is paid freelance, project-based to start, part-time. A young app whose whole voice you get to shape. The only rule: it's about real places people can actually go. What it's not: a corporate content job or an unpaid "exposure" gig.




Qualifications


  • You are original: (you bring ideas, you've got taste and a point of view).
  • You live on TikTok and it shows (you get hooks and retention in your bones).
  • You have an account that actually posts.
  • Fast in CapCut (or whatever gets it done).
  • Based in Milan, and you can go shoot on location.
  • You own the creative, but you're happy to shape strategy together with the team.




If you are interested DM us your TikTok account or one you manage!

title: Head of Marketing Fragrance & Beauty
description:
At Chanel, we are focused on creating an inclusive culture that nurtures personal growth, contributing to collective progress. We believe the uniqueness of each individual increases the diversity, complementarity and effectiveness of our teams. We strongly encourage your application, as we value the perspective, experience and potential you could bring to CHANEL.

About the role:

The US F&B Head of Marketing is entrusted with shaping and elevating CHANELâs Brand Equity in the US, for the three strategic categories - Fragrance, Skincare and Makeup, in line with Global strategies and local market realities. This visionary leader can connect modernity with heritage, promote Brand saliency and desirability while helping drive data-informed, innovative and clientâcentric growth, in a marketing landscape shaped by AI, innovation and new possibilities. Our ideal candidate is attuned with the ever-changing rules of the US market and client journeys and energized by fostering deep emotional connections between the Brand, its existing and potential clients.

This position orchestrates the full marketing ecosystem with a strategic mindset, inspiring and promoting a collaborative partnership with DCX, Brand Communication and Artistic Direction teams, locally and globally. Leading both Brand Management and Trade & Retail Marketing teams, this role ensures that every expression of the brand is coherent, elevated and culturally resonant, creating ultimate client experience at every touchpoint.

What impact you can create at CHANEL:

Brand Strategy and Brand Equity Stewardship


  • Champion Chanelâs brand equity, ensuring all initiatives reflect the Houseâs heritage, creative vision, and uncompromising standards.
  • Translate global brand strategies into locally relevant, highâimpact plans that reinforce CHANELâs local leadership in luxury fragrance and beauty, while in return promoting US market and client realities towards Global teams, to ensure that these specific needs are understood and translated into product and orchestration strategies.
  • Be the voice of the Brand to communicate CHANELâs vision and support priorities to our Retailers and Market Teams, inspiring alignment and ambition.


Category Expertise Across the Three Axis


  • Demonstrate deep strategic and operational mastery of Fragrance, Skincare, and Makeup, understanding category dynamics, competitive landscapes, and client evolving motivations, to orchestrate locally relevant activations and nourish Global MTP product plans.
  • Guide category teams to build longâterm equity while delivering shortâterm business performance, supervising and consolidating A&P budget by axis, facilitating prioritization and monitoring sales performance and ROI to adapt plans and investments in real time.
  • Commission and interpret marketing intelligence, consumer research, competitive analysis, and trend forecasting to inform strategy. Translate customer data and market insights into articulated action plans by axis, govern implementation and measure success, with the support of the Transformation Office, Insights, Brand Comm (BC) and DCX teams. Connect the dots and lead the evolution from episodic insights to AI-augmented intuition, sensing cultural shifts and market opportunities.


360Â° Marketing Orchestration and Execution


  • Conduct the orchestra, leading the endâtoâend development and implementation of integrated marketing campaigns, partnering closely with Brand Communication, Digital and Client Experience, Artistic Department and Retail teams, as well as Global partners.
  • Beyond campaigns, ensure â with the support of the DCX and BC teams â that we create relevant and always-on connection across touchpoints of the clientâs journeys (events, digital activations, retail experiences, servicesâ¦), to convey the Houseâs storytelling through elevated and innovative multiâchannel expressions, elevating client lifetime value.
  • Lead in a fluid world: operate in a composable marketing model where teams and skills reconfigure around outcomes, not just fixed roles.
  • Client Journey & Experience Expertise
  • Bring a sophisticated understanding of modern F&B client journeys across online and offline ecosystems for each product axis.
  • Partner with DCX and Retail teams to ensure that every online and offline interactionâ from digital discovery, in-store experience, events or postâpurchase engagement â feels seamless and reflects the Houseâs signature level of service.
  • Create unforgettable moments: together with the Artistic and Education teams, support the Retail to deliver extraordinary in-store and event experiences, consistent with Brandâs standards.
  • Leverage insights and data to anticipate the expectations of the diverse US clientele (esp younger targets) and shape meaningful, personalized experiences for launches and core product offer.


Leadership & Team Development


  • Inspire, mentor, and develop highâperforming teams, nurturing curiosity and innovative spirit, accountability, and crossâfunctional collaboration.
  • Foster a culture of operational excellence aligned with Chanelâs image and values, where team members also feel empowered to bring âout of the boxâ ideas to create Brand magic and unique story-telling.
  • Lead with agility and promote simplification: guide an evolving organization, where roles continuously reconfigure as AIâs impact rises. Continuously assess what to automate, augment or eliminate, focusing teams on Brand priorities and high-value work. Partner with P&O to pilot adaptive organization principles when relevant.


Strategic Partnership & Influence


  • Develop strong relationships and trust with leaders of the rest of the marketing ecosystem (DCX, AD, Brand Comm) and collaborate with global and regional stakeholders to ensure strategic alignment and bestâinâclass execution.
  • Represent the voice of the market: provide sharp insights to influence global strategy and innovation pipelines.


You are energized by:


  • Developing and executing strategic plans
  • Setting lofty goals and achieving them
  • Working in a highly collaborative environment
  • Navigating complexity and supporting consistent transformation and change focusing on efficiency
  • Collaborating with passionate teammates
  • People leadership, coaching and development
  • Creative problem solving and learning something new every day


What you will bring to the team:


  • Product sensibility across Fragrance, Makeup and Skincare with deep fluency in F&B brand equity building in a US competitive and diverse environment.
  • Visionary and strategic thinker with strong executional discipline, able to think out of the box, bring the outside in and challenge conventions while honoring brand heritage and delivering exceptional client experience.
  • Inspirational leader who attracts, develops and retains top talents.
  • Deep cultural intuition and trend curiosity.
  • Data-driven and able to transform insights into action plans
  • Holistic expertise of the modern omni-channel client journey and the psychology of premium US consumers across age pyramid, as well as understanding of client-centric communication automation tools and growing impact of digital and AI on Marketing function.
  • Strong financial acumen and budget management skills.
  • Comfortable to lead by influence in complex environments, rely on centers of expertise (Brand Comm and DCX, Global teams) while challenging and connecting the dots.


Position Logistics:


  • Partially Remote: Role requires a minimum of three days in-person office presence at NYC office
  • Minimum of 20 years of experience and 15+ years of specific marketing experience
  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to synthesize data easily
  • Experience leading teams and leaders of teams
  • Bachelorâs degree required, Masterâs degree or MBA preferred
  • Proficient use of Microsoft 365 programs is required
  • Ability to travel internationally and domestically approximately 15% -20% or as business requires
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication required with an understanding of French language preferable


Compensation:


  • The anticipated base salary range for this position is $256,100 through $400,000. Base salary is one component of the total compensation for this position. Other forms of variable pay [may/will] be offered for this position. Other components [may/will] include bonus potential, benefits, and/or perks.


Benefits and Perks:


  • Wellbeing resources include dedicated paid time off for wellbeing (2-week August Office Closure) and a Wellbeing fund
  • Family and care giving benefits (inclusive of parental leave, fertility support, MilkStork, and Care.com Membership)
  • Generous paid time off policies to include vacation, holiday, sick and volunteer days
  • 401K and other incentives
  • Robust healthcare offerings; medical, dental, vision, MDLIVE (virtual care), One Medical, Flexible Spending Accounts (Health Care & Dependent Care), Health Savings Account and Employee Assistance Program
  • Life insurance, Accidental Death & Dismemberment, Short Term Disability, Long Term Disability, Health Advocate, International Business Travel Accident & Medical, and Commuter Transit & Parking


Additional Information:

Chanel is a private company whose values are grounded in creating the conditions for people to perform at their best and feel fulfilled and confident in their work. We offer a unique work environment where individuals are encouraged to better understand the brand, the business, and motivations, so that together we can unlock the possibilities of growth. This is reflected in:

Diversity and Inclusion:


  • At CHANEL, we are intentional in promoting Diversity & Inclusion. We foster respect, empathy and dignity for all. We believe strongly that the diversity of our people across the full spectrum of human differences is essential to our organisation and the connections we have with each other and our clients.
  • We offer Employee Resource Groups in the US that are voluntary, open to all, employee-led groups formed around a shared identity or lived experience, whose aim is to foster a diverse, inclusive, and equitable community aligned with the values and missions of the communities they support.


Chanel Community:


  • CHANEL Community empowers our employees to channel their passions, talents and sense of purpose to contribute to and learn from our communities.
  • Employees are encouraged to take time off annually to volunteer through CHANEL Community. CHANEL also matches employee donations to select charitable organizations.


Sustainability:


  • CHANEL Mission 1.5Â° is our climate action plan. It is focused on transforming the business in line with the ambitions of the Paris Agreement on climate change to limit the average global mean temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius above preindustrial levels.
  • Employees are encouraged to contribute to our US Sustainability efforts at the corporate level and within the divisions, through organized taskforces and initiatives.


Arts and Culture:


  • We are committed to extending our legacy of cultural engagement. Global support includes the CHANEL Culture Fund, which supports a select group of leading art culture institutions across the globe, and the CHANEL Next Prize for the next generation of creative talent. US support currently includes Annual sponsorship of the MoMA film program, Tribeca Film Festival programs and Through Her Lens, Support of the Academy Gold Fellowship for Women and Sponsorship of the BAAND Together Dance Festival at Lincoln Center.


Fondation Chanel:


  • Since 2011, Fondation CHANELâs mission is for women and girls to be free to shape their own destiny. Through multi-year partnerships with financial and technical support, Fondation CHANEL is committed to improving the safety and autonomy of women and adolescent girls around the world, impacting over a million women and girls in its first 10 years.
  • For more information, please navigate to the Fondation Chanel website here


Career and Leadership Development:


  • We have dedicated in-house teams focused on supporting the onboarding of employees, developing leadership skills via custom programs like Imagine Chanel People, Heart of Leadership and group and individual coaching, and blended online and live classes offered on our Bloom platform and by skilled trainers, such as Shape Your Career, to develop career building skills.
  • Chanel, Inc. benefits and perks are dependent on eligibility and subject to modification by Chanel at any time

title: Designer
description:
Simon & Schuster was named to Forbes magazine list of America's Best Mid-Size Employers 2022. Simon & Schuster is a global leader in general interest publishing, dedicated to providing the best in fiction and nonfiction for readers of all ages, and in all printed, digital and audio formats. Its distinguished roster of authors includes many of the world's most popular and widely recognized writers, and winners of the most prestigious literary honors and awards. It is home to numerous well-known imprints and divisions such as Simon & Schuster, Scribner, Atria Books, Gallery Books, Pocket Books, Adams Media, Simon & Schuster Children's Publishing and Simon & Schuster Audio and international companies in Australia, Canada, India and the United Kingdom, and proudly brings the works of its authors to readers in more than 200 countries and territories. For more information visit our website at www.simonandschuster.com.

Simon & Schuster is seeking an Interior Book Designer to design and layout fiction and nonfiction books in the adult division. This person will be involved in, on average, 50 projects each year.

An Ideal Candidate

  • Is an Adobe InDesign expert and book design enthusiast
  • Is a talented designer with experience designing trade interiors
  • Is able to work at all levels, from inputting corrections to building well-structured InDesign files for their own original designs
  • Understands how their composition work impacts downstream processes, including ebook production and future print editions
  • Will work collaboratively with Production Editorial to ensure the manuscript and InDesign document are coded complementarily and coding best practices are applied rigorously from manuscript to finished InDesign files
  • Understands book production and printing standards
  • Knows the difference between match designs, reflows, conversions, reissues and is able to create all of these

Requirements

  • 2+ years of layout experience in Adobe InDesign; expert in InDesign
  • 1 year or more of experience designing original book interiors
  • Solid understanding of book composition standards
  • Ability to read and implement proofreaderâs marks
  • Experience manipulating illustrations and photos in Photoshop
  • Experience creating and manipulating vector art in Illustrator

This role is open to both hybrid and fully remote candidates.

Simon & Schuster US is an equal opportunity employer (EOE) including disability/vet. At Simon & Schuster US, the spirit of inclusion feeds into everything that we do. From employee benefits/programs and social impact outreach initiatives, we believe that opportunity, access, resources and rewards should be available to and for the benefit of all. Simon & Schuster US is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, and Veteran status.

The hiring salary range for this position applies to New York City, California, Colorado, Washington state, and most other geographies. Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage, disability benefits, tuition assistance program and PTO or, if applicable. Additionally, employees have access to our industry-first, Broad Based Ownership program, which makes all employees partners in our shared success.

Candidates hired for this or any other posted Simon & Schuster role will be employees of Simon & Schuster, LLC, subject to all policies, including the Workplace Privacy Notice, and eligible solely for the benefits plans thereof.

Our fast-growing company is seeking a dedicated Entry-Level Administrative Assistant to support the smooth operation of our daily business activities. In this role, you will assist with organizing, updating, and maintaining accurate company records and databases while ensuring information is handled efficiently and correctly.

You will also provide general administrative support across teams, helping to improve workflow and streamline internal processes. Strong computer skills, a willingness to learn, and a keen eye for detail are essential for success in this position.

This remote opportunity offers flexible working arrangements and the chance to grow within a supportive and dynamic work environment.

What Youâll Be Doing:

  • Update spreadsheets, trackers, and documentation regularly

  • Assist with scheduling, task coordination, and administrative workflows
  • Monitor and follow up on assigned tasks to ensure completion
  • Support internal teams by maintaining structured and up-to-date information

  • Clear written communication skills

  • Self-motivated and able to work independently in a remote environment

  • Opportunities for career growth in administration and operations
  • Performance-based incentives
  • Supportive and collaborative remote team environment

Why Join?

title: Sales Director Europe
location: Madrid, Madrid, Comunidad de Madrid, EspaÃ±a
description:
Who We Are

iPiD empowers financial institutions, payment service providers, and enterprises with a single global API for Know Your Payee (KYP) verificationâreducing fraud, reducing costs, staying compliant, and streamlining cross-border payments, globally.

We are a fast-growing fintech startup, founded by a global team of industry veterans from iconic organizations like SWIFT and Thomson Reuters. Supported by leading investors such as Monk's Hill Ventures, Jungle Ventures and Saison Capital, and guided by an advisory team featuring C-suite leaders from Microsoft, Commonwealth Bank, and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. Our team combines global expertise with local insights; we are present across key citiesâincluding Singapore, Hanoi, Kuala Lumpur, Mumbai, Amsterdam, Brussels, Paris, London, New York, and Dubai.

Role Overview

As Sales Director you will play a key role in driving business growth by identifying and securing new opportunities across the region. This is a high-impact commercial role for someone who thrives on building strategic relationships, opening new markets, and delivering revenue growth within the cross-border payments industry. Working closely with cross-functional teams, you will help shape our commercial strategy and expand our presence across Europe.

Core Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement commercial strategies according to company goals and objectives aiming to accelerate growth across Europe
  • Conduct market research and analysis to create detailed business plans on commercial opportunities (expansion, business development etc.)
  • Understand the requirements of existing customers to ensure their needs are being met
  • Act to acquire new customers and manage client relationships (new and existing)
  • Collaborate with and coordinate diverse teams (product management, marketing, customer success etc.)
  • Build and maintain profitable partnerships with key stakeholders
  • Monitor performance of commercial activities using key metrics and prepare reports for senior management
  • Assist in setting financial targets and budget development and monitoring

Requirements

  • Strong academic background in engineering, business, or comparable experience
  • Result-oriented with at least 5 years of experience and a track record in the payment industry, preferably cross-border payments
  • Proven experience as commercial director or other relevant role in the Europe
  • Proven experience in sales and/or marketing and managing relationships with key clients
  • In-depth understanding of market research methods and analysis
  • Solid knowledge of performance reporting and financial/budgeting processes
  • Commercial awareness partnered with a strategic mindset
  • Excellent organizational and leadership skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English and at least two major European languages (e.g., French, German, Spanish, or Italian).
  • Ability to communicate clearly and confidently with technical and business audiences
  • Candidates may be based anywhere in Europe and must have valid work authorization. Visa sponsorship is not available.

Benefits

What We Offer

  • Meaningful Impact - Play a key role in shaping the future of trusted cross-border payments and fraud prevention, helping solve critical challenges for financial institutions and businesses worldwide
  • Learn from experienced industry leaders - Join a team with deep expertise across payments, fintech and technology, and gain exposure to global customers and markets
  • Ownership & Growth - Join a fast-growing global fintech where your contributions are visible and valued, with the opportunity to participate in our Employee Stock Option Plan (ESOP) and share in our long-term success

_______________________________________________________________

Working in accordance with applicable laws, iPiD prohibits discrimination against any applicant or employee based on any legally-recognized basis, including, but not limited to: race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, childbirth or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, genetic information (including testing and characteristics), veteran status, uniformed service member status or any other status protected by national or local laws of the jurisdiction in which the applicant resides.

If you need assistance or request accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact hr@ipid.tech

Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. All information gathered will be treated in strict confidence and solely used for recruitment purposes.

____

To protect the interests of all parties, iPiD does not accept unsolicited resumes from a third party agency or search firm recruiters. Any unsolicited resumes sent to any iPiD employee, regardless of the means of transmission, will become the sole property of iPiD. We will not pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.

title: Project Management Officer
description:
Project Objectives: Support the IT Portfolio Governance function by ensuring projects are delivered on schedule, within budget, and in accordance with governance standards. The role is responsible for coordinating project governance activities, monitoring project costs and benefits, maintaining project documentation, and facilitating communication across project stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities:

Portfolio Governance

  • Maintain project governance documentation and ensure project documentation checklists are up to date.
  • Coordinate regular project meetings and prepare meeting materials.
  • Organize monthly Portfolio Steering Committee (SteerCo) meetings.
  • Monitor and review monthly project status across the portfolio.
  • Coordinate with business users, Project Managers, and internal teams to ensure effective communication and alignment.

Project Cost Management

  • Monitor project cost trackers and ensure spending remains within the approved budget.
  • Consolidate and prepare monthly project cost reports.
  • Highlight cost variances and escalate potential risks when necessary.

Project Benefit Tracking

  • Maintain project benefit documentation and repository.
  • Track, consolidate, and validate project benefit realization.
  • Coordinate monthly benefit realization meetings with relevant stakeholders.

Project Coordination & Governance

  • Ensure project activities are executed according to the agreed timeline without unnecessary scope changes.
  • Remind Team Leads of upcoming resource contract expirations to support workforce planning.
  • Support the Portfolio Governance Lead with day-to-day governance activities.
  • Perform additional ad hoc tasks based on business priorities.


Required Skills

  • Minimum 2 years of experience in IT Project Management, PMO, Project Governance, or IT Portfolio Management.
  • Strong understanding of Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), including Agile and Waterfall methodologies.
  • Experience in project governance, PMO processes, project reporting, and portfolio management.
  • Knowledge of project budgeting, cost tracking, and benefit realization.
  • Understanding of project delivery, acceptance processes, and governance within a fast-paced environment.
  • Excellent stakeholder management, coordination, and communication skills.
  • Ability to prepare executive-level reports and presentation materials.
  • Fluent in both English and Bahasa Indonesia, written and spoken.

title: Cashier
description:
Job Summary

Under general supervision, the Cashier performs clerical work in collecting payments from customers.

Because the Authority has a responsibility to serve the public 24 hours a day, an employee in this class is expected to work overtime as conditions demand and on short notice during emergency conditions.

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

  • Receives cash payments from customers in partial and full payment of deposits, service fees, bills, invoices and other fees
  • Uses cash register and keys in amounts paid; makes change as necessary
  • Receives and verifies check payments to ensure the correctness of the data, payee, payer, dollar amount, signature, and the amount written in words
  • Accepts mail payments and payments dropped in the overnight depository, and records amount paid on cash register
  • Clears cash register at the end of the day; totals stubs and receipts; counts cash and checks and compares total on cash register with currency in register to verify balances
  • Prepares deposit slips and daily cash summary showing monies collected
  • Accepts responsibility for shortages in cash collected
  • Performs other duties as assigned

Required Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

  • Thorough knowledge of regulations, practices, and procedures applicable to the collection and depositing of revenues
  • Ability to meet the character and reputation standards of a bonding company
  • Ability to keep records of cash received and to prepare a variety of routine reports on such transactions
  • Considerable knowledge of standard office practices and procedures
  • High degree of initiative and dependability
  • Some knowledge of the method and equipment used in data entry and computer utilization
  • Ability to plan work and carry through to completion without close supervision
  • Ability to use adding machines, copy machines and computer terminals
  • Ability to review documents to verify completeness and accuracy of information
  • Ability to deal effectively with all persons contacted during the course of work
  • Skill in counting money and recording transactions with speed and accuracy
  • Skill in the operation of adding machines and other mechanical office aids

Education & Experience

High School diploma, or equivalent, including or supplemented by courses in commercial subjects and four (4) years of progressively responsible experience in public contact work, three (3) years of which must have involved collecting and accounting for large sums of money.

The Virgin Islands Water & Power Authority is proud to offer a comprehensive benefits package to eligible, full time employees.

  • Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance
  • Government Employees Retirement System (GERS)
  • Basic Life and AD&D Insurance
  • Voluntary Life Insurance
  • Long Term Disability Insurance
  • Supplemental Insurance
  • Personal Time Off (PTO)
  • 457b Deferred Compensation Plan
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
